title: RT-LAMP for BYDV-PAS and BYDV-MAV detection
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"emv29","text":"A rapid test was developed primarily to support QC of lab colonies, and testing colonies prior to starting lab experiments. It could also be utilised for general BYDV testing of aphids from trap samples for rapid surveillance. An RT-LAMP assay was developed to detect the presence of Luteovirus pashordei (BYDV-PAS) and Luteovirus mavhordei (BYDV-MAV) in samples - these are the two BYDV species routinely utilised in our lab experiments.  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":284,"length":20},{"style":"italic","offset":320,"length":20}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"f18e1","text":"While the assay was designed to detect BYDV-MAV - it will also likely detect BYDV-PAV. The assay was designed in target regions to distinguish between BYDV-MAV and BYDV-PAS. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Thaw all components at RT and place on ice (WarmStart Colorimetric LAMP 2X Master Mix; 10X LAMP primer mix; Target RNA or gBlock (0.1 ng/μl) controls.

Pipette 24 µL into PCR 8-strips (e.g. 72.991.002 from Sarstedt) and add 1 µl of target RNA, gBlock, control, or water for no-template-control. Mix by pipetting up and down, and briefly centrifuge to collect contents. Confirm that reaction solutions have a bright pink color, which indicates an initial high pH, which is required for a successful pH-LAMP reaction.
Incubate at 65 °C in a thermocycler or heating block for 30 minutes.

Remove tubes or vessels from incubation and examine by eye. Positive reactions will have turned yellow while negative controls should remain pink. If color change is not robust, e.g. an orange color is visible, return reactions to 65°C for an additional 10 minutes. When extending the reaction time it is important to monitor the no-template and negative controls closely to ensure false positive reactions are not present. Color will be visible directly on removal from incubation temperature, but can be intensified by allowing the reaction to cool to room temperature.
The result can be photographed to record the colorimetric results, or simply kept at room temperature in the reaction vessel.

The primers for RT-LAMP were designed for Luteovirus mavhordei from the sequence OQ686659, and for Luteovirus pashordei from the sequence OQ686684. Primer Name Sequence (5' - 3') Scale Desalting 1 F3-MAV001 ACAGCTTCACAGAGGTCAAG 25nm STD 2 B3-MAV001 ATGGTGGCAAGAGACAGTTC 25nm STD 3 FIP-MAV001 TTCCATGATGGGCTCGAGGTGTGCCTCATGAAGGAGGTCGT 25nm STD 4 BIP-MAV001 GCCGCCTCCACCTGGATAACATCTGCGTTAAGCGTTGAGT 25nm STD 5 LF-MAV001 TCTTCTTCGCCGTTCTTCTCCT 25nm STD Primers as ordered from Integrated DNA Technologies (IDT) for Luteovirus mavhordei (BYDV-MAV) assay. Primer Name Sequence (5' - 3') Scale Desalting 1 F3-PAS001 GGGTTTTTAGAGGGGCTCTG 25nm STD 2 B3-PAS001 TGTCCCCCTTACCAACTGTA 25nm STD 3 FIP-PAS001 CGGGCTCCGTCTGTGACCTTAAGCCTCCTCCGGTTTTGAAAG 25nm STD 4 BIP-PAS001 CCTAACCCAAACCCACCTCGGCTGCAACCAAGGCATTGTG 25nm STD 5 LF-PAS001 CCGGCAGTCCTAATAGAGAAAAGG 25nm STD 6 LB-PAS001 GGTATACAAAGCCCCAAACGCC 25nm STD Primers as ordered from Integrated DNA Technologies (IDT) for Luteovirus pashordei (BYDV-PAS) assay.

A gene fragment was synthesised for each target region to act as a positive control. gBlocks.txt These were ordered from Integrated DNA Technologies and diluted to 10 ng/ μl with IDTE ( 10 millimolar (mM) Tris, 0.1 µg/µL EDTA). Working solutions for testing were prepared by carrying out serial dilutions of the stock.
A 10X LAMP primer mix was prepared for each of the two assays according to the following tables: %s %s %s %s

Prepare reaction mix according to table below for the number of target samples (plus positive control(s) and no template and/or negative control(s)): Component Amount (μl) 1 WarmStart Colorimetric LAMP 2X Master Mix 12.5 2 LAMP Primer Mix (10X) [MAV0001 or PAS0001] 2.5 3 dH2O 9 Vortex reaction mix and briefly spin contents down in centrifuge.
